reasoning: While the LLM fidelity metrics indicate excellent text and structural preservation, programmatic detectors identified a critical defect regarding header/footer retention across all 6 pages. Additionally, there is a major defect concerning mixed numbering types in lists, suggesting sub-point flattening. According to count-based routing thresholds, the presence of any critical defect requires a REJECT decision.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (6): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 6; Page 3 of 6
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6, pages_affected=6/6. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source document uses underlining for emphasis in several key places, including the 'MEMORANDUM' title and all the topic lead-ins in the bulleted lists (e.g., 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Costs'). The extraction output fails to preserve this underline formatting, rendering these text elements as plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: MEMORANDUM (underlined on page 1); Air Quality (underlined on pages 3 and 4); Beneficial Reuse (underlined on pages 3 and 4)

Fix list structures to ensure sub-points are properly nested instead of flattened.
